name: ServiceNow Threat Intelligence
description: This plugin is used to pull indicators of type MD5, SHA256, URL, IPv4, IPv6, Domain, and FQDN from the ServiceNow Threat Intelligence Observables (sn_ti_observable) table, and shares indicators of the same types back to the Observables table. The plugin supports pull retraction and push retraction of indicators. This plugin requires a ServiceNow account with access to the Threat Intelligence Observables table.

configuration.label: ServiceNow Instance URL
configuration.description: ServiceNow instance URL, for example https://<instance>.service-now.com.

configuration.label: Username
configuration.description: ServiceNow username with access to the Threat Intelligence Observables table.

configuration.label: Type of Threat Data to Pull
configuration.description: Indicator types to pull. If left blank, all supported indicator types will be retrieved.

configuration.label: Type of Finding to Pull
configuration.description: ServiceNow finding statuses to pull. If left blank, findings with all statuses will be retrieved.

configuration.label: Enable Polling
configuration.description: Enable/Disable polling indicators from ServiceNow. Disable if you only need to push indicators to ServiceNow.

configuration.label: Enable Push Retraction
configuration.description: Enable/Disable push retraction of indicator(s) from ServiceNow. When enabled, previously pushed indicator(s) to ServiceNow will be deleted when retracted in CE. Note: The observable record is permanently deleted from your Threat Intelligence Observables table.

configuration.label: Retraction Interval (in days)
configuration.description: Number of days to use as the retraction interval for ServiceNow indicator(s) retraction. This parameter applies only when IOC(s) Retraction is enabled in Threat Exchange Settings. Valid values range from 1 to 100000 days.

configuration.label: Initial Range (in days)
configuration.description: Number of days to pull the data for the initial run. Value must be between 1 and 365.
